Enough "yes" votes could grant sweeping powers to President Erdogan, radically changing Turkey's political system. There is no question on the ballot paper, just the words 'yes' and 'no' Turkey's tense referendum on constitutional amendments could greatly expand the powers of Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. The referendum in Turkey last Sunday on a constitutional reform resulted in a narrow victory ΓÇô 51.4 to 48.6 % ΓÇô for President Recep Tayyip Erdo─ƒan and his governing party, the Justice and Development Party (AKP). The 18 amendments to the constitution aim at transforming the political system in Turkey from a parliamentary to a presidential democracy with wide powers for the president. [Policy Paper] On 16 April the citizens of Turkey voted in a national referendum to amend the constitution. This will lead to a radical strengthening of the presidentΓÇÖs power. 51.4% of the voters backed the amendments. They will come into force after the next presidential and parliamentary election (scheduled for 2019). The amendments are an important step in the thorough reconstruction of the Republic of  Answer:

baseline.final_answer: Turkey held a constitutional referendum on April 16, 2017, which narrowly passed with 51.4% of the vote. This referendum aimed to transform the political system from a parliamentary democracy to a presidential system, granting significant powers to the president. The legitimacy of the outcome has been questioned, and it is seen by some as a setback for democratization in Turkey.

evaluation.feedback: The answer contains factual claims but lacks any citations. All claims must be supported by the provided documents.
